Hollier (12.7.2017): Léonard was there without any other members of his family (he went with a young Pictet to help run Pictet de Rochemont's merino sheep project). Léonard Revilliod was asked by Louis Necker to send specimens and was sent istructions on curing them. There is a letter from the Musée (as it was then) thanking him for the material received among his papers in the state archive.

Ce spécimen est entré dans les collections MHNG sous “Sciurus nigrescens” en provenance de Californie, ceci ayant permis de le placer par la suite sous “S. aureogaster”. Cependant, cette espèce néotropicale n’existe pas en Californie. Partant, il y de fortes chances qu’il s’agisse de Sciurus variegatoides goldmani se trouvant au sud-ouest du Mexique, région où H. de Saussure a également collecté plusieurs Sciurus aureogaster . Par ailleurs, le livre d’entrée ne mentionne pas que ce spécimen a été collecté par de Saussure (J. G. 2006).

Article de Roguin 1978 (Musées de Genève no 188)
Carter & Dolan (1978, p. 89) assumed that ZMB 2589 “appears to be the only  remaining syntype of Molossus mexicanus”, therefore they designated it as lectotype. Carter &  Dolan ́s assumption and lectotype designation was unjustified, because Baud already in 1977 (p.  208) listed two further syntype specimens from Saussure ́s collection in Museum d ́Histoire  Naturelle Geneve: MHNG 516.16, skin, skull (without braincase) from Ameca[meca], pied du  Popocatepetl, 8.500 ft., and MHNG 516.05, skin with skull inside, from Cofre de Perote,  Veracruz, 13.000 ft.. Saussure (1860: 285) mentioned only one specimen from “Coffre de  Perote, à 13.000 pieds d`altitude” and [unknown number of] additional specimens from “Ameca,  au pied du Popocatepetl, à une altitude de 8.500 pieds”. Thus, ZMB 2589 had come from  Ameca. All mentioned specimens are syntypes.
